mnemonics: S.T.L.D. - Seasonality, Trend, Level, Dummy Variables - The building blocks of a deterministic structural model.
keyPoints: Structural models decompose a time series into distinct, interpretable components: level, trend, seasonality, and noise.
Deterministic seasonal components use fixed patterns (e.g., dummy variables or sine/cosine waves) that repeat exactly every period.
These models assume the seasonal effect is constant in amplitude and timing over time, unlike stochastic seasonality.
commonMistakes: Assuming a deterministic seasonal model is appropriate when the seasonal pattern's amplitude is changing or evolving over time.
Forgetting to account for the loss of degrees of freedom when using seasonal dummy variables, which requires omitting one dummy to avoid the 'dummy variable trap'.
